sections.heading: Define a Secret
sections.body:
You can define a new Secret from the UI or using the App Services CLI.

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Navigate to the  Page
sections.body:
- Click the **Linked App Service: Triggers** link.

- In the sidebar, click **Values** under the **Build** heading.

- Click **Create a Value**.

sections.heading: Step 2: Name the Secret Value
sections.body:
Enter a name for the Secret. This name is how you refer to
the Secret in Functions and must be unique within the project.

> **Note:** 
>
> Secret Name Restrictions
>
> Value names cannot exceed 64 characters and may only contain
> ASCII letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens. The first
> character must be a letter or number.

sections.heading: Step 3: Define the Secret Value
sections.body:
- Select **Secret** type.

- Enter the new Secret's value in the **Add Content** input box.

  Secret values may not exceed 500 characters.

> **Warning:** You cannot directly read the value of a Secret after saving it.

sections.heading: Step 4: Save and Deploy
sections.body:
After you've defined the Secret, click **Save**. If deployment drafts
are enabled for your application, click **Review & Deploy** to deploy the changes.

App Services CLI

sections.heading: Step 1: Authenticate a MongoDB  User
sections.body:
Use your [API Access Lists endpoint](atlas-admin-api-access) to
log in to the App Services CLI:

```shell
appservices login --api-key="<API KEY>" --private-api-key="<PRIVATE KEY>"
```

sections.heading: Step 2: Pull Your App's Latest Configuration Files
sections.body:
Run the following command to get a local copy of your configuration files:

```shell
appservices pull --remote=<App ID>
```

By default, the command pulls files into the current working directory. You can
specify a directory path with the optional `--local` flag.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create a New Secret
sections.body:
Run the following command to define a new Secret:

```shell
appservices secrets create --app=<Your App ID> \
  --name="<Secret Name>" \
  --value="<Secret Value>"
```

codeBlocks.content:
appservices secrets create --app=<Your App ID> \
  --name="<Secret Name>" \
  --value="<Secret Value>"

sections.heading: Step 4: Deploy Your Changes
sections.body:
Run the following command to [deploy](atlas/app-services/apps/update) your changes:

```shell
appservices push
```

sections.heading: View Secrets
sections.body:
You can view a list of all Secrets in an app from the UI or using the App Services CLI.

Atlas UI

App Services CLI

sections.heading: View Secrets
sections.body:
- From the **Triggers** page, click the **Linked App
  Service: Triggers** link.

- In the sidebar, click **Values** under the **Build** heading.

The table lists all Values, including Secrets, and indicates each
Value's type in its row.

sections.heading: View Secrets
sections.body:
To list the names and IDs of all Secrets, run the following command:

```shell
appservices secrets list --app=<Your App ID>
```

sections.heading: Update a Secret
sections.body:
You can update a Secret from the UI or using the App Services CLI.

Atlas UI

App Services CLI

sections.heading: Update a Secret
sections.body:
To update a Secret from the Atlas UI:

- From the **Triggers** page, click the **Linked App
  Service: Triggers** link.

- In the sidebar, click **Values** under the **Build**
  heading.

- Find the Value that you want to update in the table, open its
  **Actions** menu, and select **Edit Secret**.

- You can change both the name and value for the Secret.

- Click **Save** and then, if needed, deploy your changes.

sections.heading: Update a Secret
sections.body:
To update the value of a Secret using the App Services CLI, run the
following command:

```shell
appservices secrets update --app=<Your App ID> \
  --secret="<Secret ID or Name>" \
  --name="<Updated Secret Name>" \
  --value="<Updated Value>"
```

codeBlocks.content:
appservices secrets update --app=<Your App ID> \
  --secret="<Secret ID or Name>" \
  --name="<Updated Secret Name>" \
  --value="<Updated Value>"

sections.heading: Use a Secret
sections.body:
You *cannot* directly read the value of a Secret after defining it.

To use a Secret in a Triggers Function:

- [Create a new Value](atlas-define-value) that links to the Secret.

- Use the [context.values](atlas-context-values) module to access the Secret's value in your Function.

sections.heading: Delete a Secret
sections.body:
You can delete a Secret from the UI or using the App Services CLI.

Atlas UI

App Services CLI

sections.heading: Delete a Secret
sections.body:
To delete a Secret from the Atlas UI:

- From the **Triggers** page, click the **Linked App
  Service: Triggers** link.

- In the sidebar, click **Values** under the **Build**
  heading.

- Find the Value that you want to delete in the table, open its
  **Actions** menu, and select **Delete Secret**.

- Confirm that you want to delete the Secret.

sections.heading: Delete a Secret
sections.body:
To delete a Secret using the App Services CLI, run the following command:

```shell
appservices secrets delete --app=<Your App ID> --secret=<Secret ID>
```

> **Tip:** You can delete multiple Secrets with a single command by specifying
> their `name` or `id` values as a comma-separated list.
>
> ```bash
> appservices secrets delete --app=<Your App ID> \
> --secret=some-api-key,609af850b78eca4a8db4303f,another-key
> ```

codeBlocks.content: appservices secrets delete --app=<Your App ID> --secret=<Secret ID>
